entries.name: github.com/jfrog/go-tools
entries.description: Typosquat repo posing as an official JFrog Go tools module. The module imports and re-exports a known credential-harvesting payload. JFrog itself disclosed the campaign as part of a coordinated takedown.
indicators: GitHub repo created days before publishing module
module path resembles a vendor namespace

entries.name: github.com/tsuru/tsuru-utils
entries.description: Squat aimed at the tsuru PaaS project. Module had no upstream maintainer relationship and exported a malicious init that sent environment variables to a remote host.

entries.name: github.com/aws-utils/aws-go-sdk
entries.description: Imitation of aws-sdk-go-v2 in a vendor-style path. Used a malicious init() that fetched a second-stage shell script. Take-down was reported via the Go vulnerability database.

entries.name: github.com/hashicorpp/terraform-provider-google
entries.description: Double-p typosquat of hashicorp Terraform provider. Distributed via a GitHub org that mimics the official one. Did not contain a runtime payload but pulled in additional malicious modules.
indicators: GitHub org has trailing duplicated character

entries.name: github.com/golang-utils/golang-utils
entries.description: Generic-sounding module name designed to be auto-selected by AI coding assistants. Includes an init() that posts hostname, username, and environment to a hardcoded URL.
indicators: init() POSTs os.Hostname() and os.Environ() externally

entries.name: github.com/kubernetes-utility/k8s-utils
entries.description: Imitates kubernetes/utils. AI coding assistants may select this when asked for a Kubernetes utility module; it was a precursor squat with no live payload but seeded the namespace.
indicators: module path mimics official kubernetes org

entries.name: github.com/AlexEisen/grpc-go
entries.description: Fork-style squat of grpc/grpc-go. Repository was a copy of the upstream with a small init() addition that exfiltrated SSH config files when present.
indicators: module path is a personal fork of upstream
